events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need parse scenario. Field service high voltage safety, observed condition: insulation resistance test skipped after repair. Work rough-complete. Need identify component or condition and visible defect cues. Decision fail because dangerous/non-compliant. Risk high/critical? Skipping insulation resistance test after HV repair can lead to undetected insulation breakdown, shock, arc flash. Critical maybe. Work stage rough-complete. Component conditions installed-defective? The component is high-voltage system / insulation; condition installed-defective because test skipped, potential latent defect. Also non-compliant. Need findings: missing insulation resistance test, unverified dielectric integrity, possible hidden insulation damage, non-compliance with OEM HV procedures and NFPA 70E. Actions: perform insulation resistance test per OEM procedure, verify values within spec, document, ensure HV interlock/lockout, re-test before energizing. Rationale. References: OEM HV procedures, NFPA 70E (electrical safety, LOTO, testing), maybe IEEE 43. Need numeric fields: percent_complete maybe 80? value 0 (no reading). confidence 0.95. event_time 0, rate 0. order empty. workable empty. sound_source empty string. Ensure valid JSON.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 80,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Insulation resistance test was skipped after high-voltage repair",
    "Dielectric integrity of HV cables, connectors, and components is unverified",
    "Latent insulation damage or contamination may remain undetected",
    "Condition is non-compliant with OEM HV service procedures and electrical safety practice",
    "Energizing the asset without verification creates shock and arc-flash exposure"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work and secure the HV system under lockout/tagout",
    "Perform insulation resistance testing per OEM HV procedure for the repaired circuit",
    "Compare measured values to OEM specification and minimum acceptable megohm limits",
    "Inspect HV cables, connectors, and boots for damage, contamination, or improper seating",
    "Document test results and clear the lockout only after passing values are confirmed",
    "Re-test if any component is disturbed during inspection"
  ],
  "rationale": "After a high-voltage repair, the insulation resistance test is the primary means of confirming that dielectric barriers are intact and that the assembly is safe to energize. Skipping it leaves a latent, potentially life-threatening defect in place, so the work must fail inspection until the test is completed and documented.",
  "references": [
    "OEM HV procedures",
    "NFPA 70E-adjacent electrical safety and verification practices"
  ]
}
